text:
In a quiet town somewhere deep within the lush hills of England stood an old village church. It had stood there longest among the oldest stories, whispered about in oak-paneled halls centuries ago. That was until an ambitious young architect moved there.

Lady Eleanor was fascinated by stories. Every stone curtsy seemed a tale waiting to come alive. Her dreams were grandiose.

Her latest project? Something majestic that would blend seamlessly with mother nature. She talked her way into gaining permission.

A little persuasion later…and there it was.

A little wooden ship named Alice.

An escape ship from Victorian fiction.

Every Sunday, it bobbed gently towards the lake.

The church folk loved their talking ship...

And they loved eccentrically devoted Lady Eleanor.

One day they found her counting coins under her roof.

"Alice thinks she talks?"

They laughed.

"My dear, it’s just...peculiar spending?"

"I believe it?”

"Oh. Well.”

Eleanor was crestfallenic.

"The Owl and Fox?"

"Hush
